title: Recognizing that article I, section 10 of the United States Constitution explicitly reserves to the States the sovereign power to repel an invasion and defend their citizenry from the overwhelming and "imminent danger" posed by paramilitary, narco-terrorist cartels, terrorists and criminal actors who seized control of our southern border.
plain_summary: This congressional resolution says that states have the right to keep out people who are not supposed to be there. It claims that from 2021 to 2024, border states along Mexico were invaded by terrorists, drug cartels, and criminals. The resolution argues that these states have the constitutional power to defend themselves against this invasion without waiting for the federal government. It includes many statistics about illegal border crossings and crimes to support its argument that the Biden administration failed to protect these states.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution recognizes that Article One, Section Ten of the United States Constitution gives states the power to fight back against an invasion and protect their citizens from serious and immediate danger. The resolution says this danger comes from armed criminal groups, drug cartels that use violence like terrorists, actual terrorists, and other criminals who have taken control of our southern border.

sections.title: Background Facts
sections.text: The resolution lists many facts to support its argument. It says the Constitution requires the federal government to keep peace at home and defend the country. It also requires the federal government to protect each state from invasion. The resolution claims President Biden failed to do his job and follow the law. From twenty twenty-one through twenty twenty-four, there were record-breaking numbers of people caught crossing the southern border illegally. Many months had more than two hundred thousand people caught, and the numbers kept growing. Border states spent billions of dollars each year to provide healthcare, housing, education, and other services for people who entered the country illegally. These people came from over one hundred sixty countries, including some individuals on the terrorist watch list and people from countries of special concern. Thousands of crimes were committed by people who entered illegally, including assault, murder, drug crimes, trafficking, and sexual crimes. From twenty twenty-one through twenty twenty-four, crimes by people who entered illegally increased by more than one hundred percent each year. Arrests of people who entered illegally and were convicted of sex crimes increased by more than one thousand percent each year according to Customs and Border Protection. Authorities seized thousands of pounds of fentanyl, which is enough to kill every American multiple times. The United States has a drug problem where tens of thousands of Americans die every year, and almost one hundred percent of drugs like heroin, methamphetamine, fentanyl, and cocaine are smuggled across the southern border. Women and children trying to cross the border were abused, raped, tortured, enslaved, and trafficked because the Biden administration failed to secure the southern border. From twenty twenty-one through twenty twenty-four, attempts to cross by criminals and terrorists on the FBI terror watch list increased greatly, and even more may have crossed without being caught. Criminal cartels act like armed military groups and terrorists that make money from moving people and drugs across the southern border into the United States. Customs and Border Protection agents have confirmed that cartels help with and profit from almost every illegal border crossing. The Biden administration removed effective measures that reduced illegal immigration and secured the border, including programs that made people wait in Mexico and building border walls. The Biden administration put in place measures that increased illegal immigration, including bringing back catch and release policies, making asylum requirements weaker, and many other policy decisions that encourage people to ignore our laws and make cartels bolder. States along the southern border, which are Texas, New Mexico, Arizona, and California, are forced to spend billions of their own taxpayer dollars to deal with the problems caused by an unsecured southern border. From twenty twenty-one through twenty twenty-four, state and local police were overwhelmed by criminal activity related to illegal border crossings, putting officers and communities in danger while using up local resources. From twenty twenty-one through twenty twenty-four, the quality of life and safety of communities in border states and every other state were threatened, including ranchers who had property stolen and damaged, healthcare providers with too many patients, and teachers struggling to handle the high number of migrant children in classrooms.

sections.title: House of Representatives Declarations
sections.text: The House of Representatives declares and finds several things as facts. First, any state in the United States has the right as a sovereign power to keep out of its territory any person who does not have the right to be there. Second, from twenty twenty-one through twenty twenty-four, the states at our southern border, including Texas, New Mexico, Arizona, and California, were invaded or were in such immediate danger from armed criminal cartels, drug cartels that use terrorist methods, terrorists, and criminal actors that waiting would be too dangerous. Article One, Section Ten of the United States Constitution gives these states the sovereign and one-sided authority to defend themselves against the invasion or immediate danger. Third, from twenty twenty-one through twenty twenty-four, the United States failed to protect these southern border states against invasion as required by Article Four, Section Four of the United States Constitution. The federal government also failed to ensure domestic peace, provide for the common defense, and execute the laws of the United States. Fourth, the Biden administration failed to faithfully execute the laws of the United States and to defend and protect the southern border states against invasion or immediate danger.
